package_description: Electricity-only grinding-system efficiency upgrade on non-CCS cement methods.
rollout_limit_notes: Grinding upgrades depend on major mill replacement or refurbishment cycles, so rollout should be materially slower than software-only optimisation packages.
evidence_summary: Cement grinding upgrades are the cleanest accepted pure-efficiency overlay because they are electricity-only and avoid implicitly recreating clinker-factor or fuel-switch changes.
derivation_method: Use a conservative electricity-only multiplier near the middle of the accepted band so the package remains within the indicative 3 to 7 kWh/t cement-equivalent savings range.
review_notes: Do not apply to CCS. Shares the cement non-stacking group with kiln AI process optimisation.

package_description: Operational kiln and plant-control optimisation package on non-CCS cement methods.
rollout_limit_notes: Operational optimisation can scale faster than heavy grinding retrofits, but keep the role-level cap moderate because persistence depends on data quality, ongoing tuning, and plant operating discipline.
evidence_summary: Cement kiln-control optimisation is the strongest operational cement package because it is route-preserving and materially smaller than the broader clinker-factor and fuel-switch pathway changes.
derivation_method: Use a blended conservative multiplier on thermal fuels plus a smaller electricity effect so the package stays within the accepted modest operational range and below the full CIF pathway improvement band.
review_notes: Do not apply to CCS. Shares the cement non-stacking group with the grinding-system upgrade.
